events.description: Defendant No. 2 executed a document styled 'Kararkharedi' (mortgage by conditional sale) in favour of defendant No. 1 for Rs. 1,500 and delivered possession, with a stipulation that if the amount was returned before 15th March 1973 the property would be given back
Notice sent by defendant No. 2 to defendant No. 1 (after defendant No. 1 did not accept the tendered amount)
Defendant No. 2 sent the amount to defendant No. 1 by money order, which was refused by defendant No. 1
Defendant No. 2 transferred the suit land to the plaintiff for Rs. 5,000 by registered sale deed
Defendant No. 2 filed a one-line written statement in the trial court admitting the plaintiff's suit, supported by an affidavit of the same date
Trial Court decreed the plaintiff's suit for redemption of the mortgage by conditional sale
Under the preliminary decree, the plaintiff was ordered to pay Rs. 1,500 into court on or before 29.10.1975 (or any extended date), upon which defendant No. 1 was to reconvey the property
Lower Appellate Court upheld the Trial Court's decree by its judgment

High Court, by the impugned judgment in second appeal, set aside both lower court judgments and directed restoration of the land to defendant No. 2 with instalment repayments of Rs. 800 to defendant No. 1 and Rs. 500 to the plaintiff
Supreme Court allowed the appeal, set aside the High Court's judgment, and restored the judgment and decree of the Trial Court as upheld by the Lower Appellate Court, without costs

facts.value: Plaintiff/appellant: Vidhyadhar Vishnupant Ratnaparkhi; Defendant No. 1: Manikrao Babarao Deshmukh (mortgagee/contesting respondent); Defendant No. 2: Pandu Ganu Bhalerao (original owner/vendor)
R.C.S. No. 195/73 in the Court of Civil Judge Senior Division, Buldana
4.04 acres of land of survey plot No. 15 of Kasba Amdapur, District Buldana (whole plot measures 16.09 acres)
Redemption of the mortgage by conditional sale, or in the alternative a decree for specific performance of the contract for repurchase
Kararkharedi (mortgage) of 24.3.1971 for Rs. 1,500, repayable before 15.3.1973; registered sale deed of 19.6.1973 in plaintiff's favour for Rs. 5,000
Section 58(c) Transfer of Property Act (mortgage by conditional sale), Sections 54 and 55(4)(b) TP Act (sale and unpaid seller's charge), Section 100 CPC (second appeal), Order 16 CPC (witnesses)

A party who does not enter the witness box to state his own case on oath and avoids cross-examination invites an adverse presumption that his case is not correct; non-payment of part of the sale price does not invalidate a sale under Section 54 TP Act once title has passed
Appeal allowed; High Court judgment set aside; Trial Court decree for redemption (as upheld by the Lower Appellate Court) restored; no order as to costs

doc_index.title: Vidhyadhar vs Manikrao & Anr, Supreme Court of India, judgment dated 17 March 1999

notes: DRAFT — annotated by AI from the judgment text; REQUIRES HUMAN REVIEW before use as ship-gate ground truth. Source: Indian Kanoon https://indiankanoon.org/doc/1332419/ (text re-rendered to a text-based PDF; page numbers refer to the rendered PDF, not the official court copy). Ambiguities: (1) issue No. 4 as framed by the Trial Court (page 2) says 'prior to 15.3.71', apparently a typo for the 15.3.1973 repurchase deadline stipulated in the Kararkharedi (page 1); (2) the exact date of institution of the suit is not stated (suit number indicates 1973); (3) the 17.3.1999 judgment date appears only in the Indian Kanoon header on page 1, while the operative outcome is on page 14; (4) the key_holding on adverse presumption is developed across pages 4-5 — page 4 is cited as where the finding on defendant No. 1 avoiding the witness box appears.
